audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's direct complaint to God: bitterness of soul, objection to condemnation without explanation, creation/body imagery, hidden divine purpose, and plea for a little relief before death-shadow.

verses.restored: My soul loathes my life; I will give free course to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
I will say to the Divine, Do not declare me wicked; make me know why you contend with me.
Is it good for you to oppress, to reject the labor of your hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?
Do you have eyes of flesh? Do you see as a mortal sees?
Are your days like the days of a mortal, or your years like the days of a strong man,
that you seek out my guilt and search for my sin,
though you know that I am not wicked, and there is no one who can rescue from your hand?
Your hands shaped me and made me, together all around; yet you would swallow me up.
Remember, please, that you made me like clay; will you return me to dust?
Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
You clothed me with skin and flesh, and wove me with bones and sinews.
Life and covenant-kindness you gave me, and your visitation guarded my spirit.
Yet these things you hid in your heart; I know that this was with you.
If I sinned, you would watch me, and you would not acquit me from my guilt.
If I am wicked, woe to me; and if I am righteous, I cannot lift my head, filled with shame and seeing my affliction.
If my head rises, you hunt me like a lion and again show yourself wonderful against me.
You renew your witnesses against me and increase your vexation with me; changes and an army are with me.
Why then did you bring me out from the womb? I would have expired, and no eye would have seen me.
I would have been as though I had not been, carried from womb to grave.
Are not my days few? Stop, turn from me, so I may brighten a little,
before I go and do not return, to the land of darkness and deep shadow,
a land of gloom like thick darkness, of deep shadow without order, where light is like thick darkness.
